title: Pressure-Packed Coriander Crumble
description: A show-off British dish featuring fermented coriander leaves, perfect for low-carb enthusiasts and those looking to impress.
instructions: Preheat your pressure cooker to a safe temperature, usually around 15psi for about 10 minutes.
In a mixing bowl, combine the fermented coriander leaves with coconut oil and almond flour. Mix well until there are no lumps remaining.
Fold in vanilla extract until fully incorporated into the mixture.
Transfer the crumble to your pre-heated pressure cooker lid.
Close the pressure cooker and cook for about 10 minutes on high pressure or as per manufacturer's instructions.
Allow it to naturally release steam before opening, ensuring all heat is off once done.
